chapter_title.ar: باب مَوْلِدِ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/277)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/277)

parts.text: عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ

text: إِنَّ فَاطِمَةَ بِنْتَ أَسَدٍ أُمَّ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ كَانَتْ أَوَّلَ امْرَأَةٍ هَاجَرَتْ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مِنْ مَكَّةَ إِلَى الْمَدِينَةِ عَلَى قَدَمَيْهَا وَ كَانَتْ مِنْ أَبَرِّ النَّاسِ بِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله )

فَسَمِعَتْ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَ هُوَ يَقُولُ إِنَّ النَّاسَ يُحْشَرُونَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ عُرَاةً كَمَا وُلِدُوا فَقَالَتْ وَا سَوْأَتَاهْ فَقَالَ لَهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَإِنِّي أَسْأَلُ اللَّهَ أَنْ يَبْعَثَكِ كَاسِيَةً

وَ سَمِعَتْهُ يَذْكُرُ ضَغْطَةَ الْقَبْرِ فَقَالَتْ وَا ضَعْفَاهْ فَقَالَ لَهَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَإِنِّي أَسْأَلُ اللَّهَ أَنْ يَكْفِيَكِ ذَلِكِ وَ قَالَتْ لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) يَوْماً إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ أُعْتِقَ جَارِيَتِي هَذِهِ فَقَالَ لَهَا إِنْ فَعَلْتِ أَعْتَقَ اللَّهُ بِكُلِّ عُضْوٍ مِنْهَا عُضْواً مِنْكِ مِنَ النَّارِ

فَلَمَّا مَرِضَتْ أَوْصَتْ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ أَمَرَتْ أَنْ يُعْتِقَ خَادِمَهَا وَ اعْتُقِلَ لِسَانُهَا فَجَعَلَتْ تُومِي إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) إِيمَاءً فَقَبِلَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَصِيَّتَهَا فَبَيْنَمَا هُوَ ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ قَاعِدٌ إِذْ أَتَاهُ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) وَ هُوَ يَبْكِي فَقَالَ لَهُ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مَا يُبْكِيكَ فَقَالَ مَاتَتْ أُمِّي فَاطِمَةُ فَقَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ وَ أُمِّي وَ اللَّهِ وَ قَامَ مُسْرِعاً حَتَّى دَخَلَ

فَنَظَرَ إِلَيْهَا وَ بَكَى ثُمَّ أَمَرَ النِّسَاءَ أَنْ يَغْسِلْنَهَا وَ قَالَ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) إِذَا فَرَغْتُنَّ فَلَا تُحْدِثْنَ شَيْئاً حَتَّى تُعْلِمْنَنِي فَلَمَّا فَرَغْنَ أَعْلَمْنَهُ بِذَلِكَ فَأَعْطَاهُنَّ أَحَدَ قَمِيصَيْهِ الَّذِي يَلِي جَسَدَهُ وَ أَمَرَهُنَّ أَنْ يُكَفِّنَّهَا فِيهِ وَ قَالَ لِلْمُسْلِمِينَ إِذَا رَأَيْتُمُونِي قَدْ فَعَلْتُ شَيْئاً لَمْ أَفْعَلْهُ قَبْلَ ذَلِكَ فَسَلُونِي لِمَ فَعَلْتُهُ

فَلَمَّا فَرَغْنَ مِنْ غُسْلِهَا وَ كَفْنِهَا دَخَلَ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَحَمَلَ جَنَازَتَهَا عَلَى عَاتِقِهِ فَلَمْ يَزَلْ تَحْتَ جَنَازَتِهَا حَتَّى أَوْرَدَهَا قَبْرَهَا ثُمَّ وَضَعَهَا وَ دَخَلَ الْقَبْرَ فَاضْطَجَعَ فِيهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ فَأَخَذَهَا عَلَى يَدَيْهِ حَتَّى وَضَعَهَا فِي الْقَبْرِ ثُمَّ انْكَبَّ عَلَيْهَا طَوِيلًا يُنَاجِيهَا وَ يَقُولُ لَهَا ابْنُكِ ابْنُكِ [ابْنُكِ]

ثُمَّ خَرَجَ وَ سَوَّى عَلَيْهَا ثُمَّ انْكَبَّ عَلَى قَبْرِهَا فَسَمِعُوهُ يَقُولُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَسْتَوْدِعُهَا إِيَّاكَ ثُمَّ انْصَرَفَ فَقَالَ لَهُ الْمُسْلِمُونَ إِنَّا رَأَيْنَاكَ فَعَلْتَ أَشْيَاءَ لَمْ تَفْعَلْهَا قَبْلَ الْيَوْمِ فَقَالَ الْيَوْمَ فَقَدْتُ بِرَّ أَبِي طَالِبٍ إِنْ كَانَتْ لَيَكُونُ عِنْدَهَا الشَّيْ‏ءُ فَتُؤْثِرُنِي بِهِ عَلَى نَفْسِهَا وَ وَلَدِهَا

وَ إِنِّي ذَكَرْتُ الْقِيَامَةَ وَ أَنَّ النَّاسَ يُحْشَرُونَ عُرَاةً فَقَالَتْ وَا سَوْأَتَاهْ فَضَمِنْتُ لَهَا أَنْ يَبْعَثَهَا اللَّهُ كَاسِيَةً وَ ذَكَرْتُ ضَغْطَةَ الْقَبْرِ فَقَالَتْ وَا ضَعْفَاهْ فَضَمِنْتُ لَهَا أَنْ يَكْفِيَهَا اللَّهُ ذَلِكَ فَكَفَّنْتُهَا بِقَمِيصِي وَ اضْطَجَعْتُ فِي قَبْرِهَا لِذَلِكَ وَ انْكَبَبْتُ عَلَيْهَا فَلَقَّنْتُهَا مَا تُسْأَلُ عَنْهُ فَإِنَّهَا سُئِلَتْ عَنْ رَبِّهَا فَقَالَتْ وَ سُئِلَتْ عَنْ رَسُولِهَا فَأَجَابَتْ وَ سُئِلَتْ عَنْ وَلِيِّهَا وَ إِمَامِهَا فَارْتَجَّ عَلَيْهَا فَقُلْتُ ابْنُكِ ابْنُكِ [ابْنُكِ] .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Muhammad Bin Abdullah, from Al Sayyari, from Muhammad Bin Jamhour, from some of our companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Fatima<sup>asws</sup> Bint Asad<sup>as</sup> is the mother of Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>. She<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> was the first woman to emigrate to Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, from Makkah to Al-Medina upon her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> feet, and she<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> was the most righteous of the people with Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>.

So she<sup>as</sup> heard Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> and he<sup>saww</sup> was saying: ‘The people would be Resurrected on the Day of Judgment, naked just as they were born’. So she<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> said: ‘Oh the evil of it!’ So Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said to her: ‘So I<sup>saww</sup> shall ask Allah<sup>azwj</sup> that He<sup>azwj</sup> should Resurrect you<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> clothed’.

And she<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> heard him<sup>saww</sup> mention the compression of the grave, so she<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> said: ‘Oh the weakness from it!’ So Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said to her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup>: ‘So I<sup>saww</sup> shall ask Allah<sup>azwj</sup> that He<sup>azwj</sup> Restrain that’. And she<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> said to Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> one day, ‘I<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> intend to free this maid of mine’. So he<sup>saww</sup> said to her<sup>asws</sup>: ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Free, with every body part of hers, a body part of yours<sup>as</sup>, from the Fire’.

When she<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> was ill, she<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> bequeathed to Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> and instructed that he<sup>saww</sup> should free her<sup>as</sup> servant, and her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> tongue seized up, so she<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> went on gesturing to Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> with gestures. So Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> accepted her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> bequest. So while he<sup>saww</sup> was seated one day, when Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> came over and he<sup>asws</sup> was weeping. So Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said to him<sup>asws</sup>: ‘What makes you<sup>asws</sup> weep?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘My<sup>asws</sup> mother<sup>as</sup> Fatima<sup>asws</sup> passed away’. So Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘And (she<sup>as</sup> was) my<sup>saww</sup> mother (as well), by Allah<sup>azwj</sup>!’, and he<sup>saww</sup> stood up quickly until he<sup>saww</sup> went over.

So he<sup>saww</sup> looked at her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> and wept. Then he<sup>saww</sup> instructed the women that they should wash her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> and said: ‘When you are free, so do not do anything new until you let me<sup>saww</sup> know’. So when they were free, they let him<sup>saww</sup> know of that, so He<sup>saww</sup> gave them one of his<sup>saww</sup> shirts which was beneath his<sup>saww</sup> body, and instructed them that they should enshroud her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> in it, and said to the Muslims: ‘When you see me<sup>saww</sup> to have done something I<sup>saww</sup> have not done before that, so ask me<sup>as</sup> why I<sup>saww</sup> have done it’.

So when they were free from washing her<sup>as</sup> and enshrouding her<sup>as</sup>, he<sup>saww</sup> came over and carried her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> coffin upon his<sup>saww</sup> shoulder. So he<sup>saww</sup> did not cease to be beneath her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> coffin until he<sup>saww</sup> came near her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> grave, and placed it and entered her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> grave. So he<sup>saww</sup> laid down in it’. Then he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So he<sup>saww</sup> took her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> upon his<sup>saww</sup> hands until he<sup>saww</sup> placed her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> in the grave. Then he<sup>saww</sup> devoted upon her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> for a long time, whispering to her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup>, and he<sup>saww</sup> was saying to her: ‘Your<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> son<sup>asws</sup>! Your<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> son<sup>asws</sup>! Your<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> son<sup>asws</sup>!’.

Then he<sup>saww</sup> came out and evened (the soil) upon her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup>. Then he<sup>saww</sup> devoted upon her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> grave and he<sup>saww</sup> was heard saying: ‘There is no god Except Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! I<sup>saww</sup> hereby entrust her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> to You<sup>azwj</sup>’. Then he<sup>saww</sup> left. So the Muslims said, ‘We saw you<sup>saww</sup> doing things which you<sup>saww</sup> did not do those before today’. So he<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘Today I<sup>saww</sup> lost the righteousness of Abu Talib<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup>. If she<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> ever happened to have anything with her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup>, so she<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> would prefer me<sup>saww</sup> with it upon her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> own self, and her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> sons<sup>as</sup>.

And I<sup>saww</sup> mentined the Day of Judgment that the people would be Resurrected naked, so she<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> said: ‘O the evil of it!’. So I<sup>saww</sup> guaranteed to her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> that Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Resurrect her<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> clothed. And I<sup>saww</sup> mentioned the compression of the grave, so she<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> said: ‘O the weakness from it!’ So I<sup>saww</sup> guaranteed to her<sup>asws</sup> that Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Restrain that. So I<sup>saww</sup> enshrouded her<sup>asws</sup> with my<sup>saww</sup> shirt, and I<sup>saww</sup> laid down in her<sup>asws</sup> grave for that and devoted upon her<sup>as</sup>, so I<sup>saww</sup> indoctrinated her<sup>as</sup> (Talqeen) what she<sup>saww</sup> would be question about. So she<sup>saww</sup> was questioned about her<sup>asws</sup> Lord<sup>azwj</sup>, so she<sup>asws</sup> said it, and she<sup>asws</sup> was asked about her<sup>asws</sup> Rasool<sup>saww</sup>, so she<sup>asws</sup> answered, and she<sup>asws</sup> was asked about her<sup>asws</sup> Gardian<sup>asws</sup> and her<sup>asws</sup> Imam<sup>asws</sup>. So she<sup>asws</sup> was shaken over it, so I<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘Your<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> son<sup>asws</sup>! Your<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> son<sup>asws</sup>! Your<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> son<sup>asws</sup>!’.
en.sarwar: 1. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Muhammad ibn Yahya al-Farisiy from abu Hanifa Muhammad ibn Yahya from al-Walid ibn Aban from Muhammad ibn ‘Abdallah ibn Muskan from who has said the following. “Abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) has said, ‘Once Fatima, daughter of Asad came to abu Talib with the glad news of the birth of the Holy Prophet (s.a). Abu Talib said, “Wait for a sabt then I will give a similar glad news except prophet-hood.” The Imam (a.s.) said, “A sabt is thirty years. Amir al-Mu’minin Ali (a.s.) was born thirty years after the birth of the Holy Prophet (s.a).”
